message:
Last week our power went out around dinner time—no warning, no idea how long it'd last. My first instinct was panic (hello, full freezer), but then I remembered I'd stashed a couple of those hand-crank flashlights in the kitchen drawer a few months back. Grabbed one, cranked it a few times, and suddenly we had light without burning through batteries we didn't have extras of.

Turned into kind of a fun evening, actually—we made sandwiches by that light, the kids thought it was an adventure, and I wasn't white-knuckling my phone battery wondering when the juice would die. Small thing, but it genuinely made a difference.

I think the win was less about "being prepared" in some grand way and more about just having *one* thing on hand that actually worked when we needed it. It gave us breathing room to think straight.

Do you have a flashlight or two tucked somewhere easy to grab? What's helped you most during an outage?
